title: Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 is a law aimed at addressing the intersection of aging and artificial intelligence. The exact scope of the law is unknown as a detailed description is not available, but it likely deals with policy and regulations concerning AI use in aging populations.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating the use of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. The exact details of the policy are not provided, but it likely outlines rules and guidelines for the use of AI in SBA operations.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: A moratorium is being imposed on the construction of new artificial intelligence data centers, meaning no new ones can be built. This act aims to regulate or halt the development of AI data centers.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy document aims to amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to establish a prize competition program for artificial intelligence. It seeks to implement these changes for the purposes of advancing AI research.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to protect national security and interests from potential threats posed by artificial intelligence. It likely includes measures to define AI threats, establish defense protocols, and regulate AI development.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act aims to establish a system for tracking and assessing heat-related emergencies using artificial intelligence technology. This system likely seeks to provide critical support during heatwaves by efficiently monitoring and responding to heat emergencies.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.
summary: This policy establishes a pilot program using artificial intelligence to detect anomalies at US land border ports along the Arizona border. The program aims to assess the effectiveness of artificial intelligence in customs and border protection.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act aims to support and enable small-scale artificial intelligence innovators, likely through policy changes or support systems. However, without a description, the specific goals and mechanisms of this act are unclear.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a proposed policy aiming to regulate and guide the development and use of artificial intelligence. The details of the policy are unknown but imply it aims to create a framework for governing AI.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 aims to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in elections to prevent fraudulent activities. The act seeks to ensure the integrity and authenticity of election processes by setting rules and guidelines for AI applications.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy document is likely related to a law governing the development and use of advanced technology and artificial intelligence. It probably outlines the rules, regulations, and framework for managing AI within a specific department or government entity.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address and mitigate the environmental impacts of Artificial Intelligence. The act likely focuses on regulating or reducing the ecological consequences caused by AI technology.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The American Leadership in AI Act aims to promote and maintain US leadership in the field of artificial intelligence. Its purpose is to address the development and use of AI technologies in a way that benefits the country.

title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act aims to establish a framework for companies to disclose the use of artificial intelligence in products and services. This act will allow consumers to make informed decisions about AI-powered products.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This policy document aims to protect families from rising costs associated with the energy consumption of Artificial Intelligence data centers. The goal is to create legislation that mitigates the financial burden these costs may place on families.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address the environmental consequences of artificial intelligence technology. This act likely seeks to establish regulations or guidelines for the development and usage of AI to minimize its negative effects on the environment.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a policy document that aims to establish guidelines and regulations for the development and use of artificial intelligence (AI) across various sectors. It likely outlines specific rules and standards for the deployment and oversight of AI technologies to ensure their safe and responsible use.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a proposed law that aims to regulate and hold accountable technology companies for the decisions made by their algorithms. The law would likely require these companies to be transparent about how their algorithms work, and to take responsibility for any harm caused by them.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to regulate and manage potential risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence. It likely sets guidelines to ensure AI development is safe, secure, and transparent.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: This act aims to promote the development and use of artificial intelligence in the financial services sector. It likely seeks to create a framework or regulations that allow for greater innovation within the field.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is a policy document that likely focuses on the growth and advancement of artificial intelligence in China. Its purpose and details are not specified, but the title suggests a report is to be created about China's AI power.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is an act that regulates the use and development of artificial intelligence models. Its specifics are currently unavailable due to lack of description.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy aimed at creating trust in the use of artificial intelligence by supporting and developing a skilled workforce. The exact details and scope of the policy are not available.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at preparing elementary and high school students for an increasingly automated world through education and training in artificial intelligence. The act focuses on teaching AI literacy and skills to ensure students are equipped for the future workforce.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act is a proposed policy aimed at preventing the censorship of artificial intelligence-generated content. The policy's purpose is unclear without further information, but its title suggests it is related to protecting AI output from restrictions on free speech.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act is a policy aimed at evaluating the potential threat of terrorism posed by advanced artificial intelligence technology. It likely aims to assess and mitigate the risks associated with generative AI, such as its use in malicious activities.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This policy aims to restrict how employers use automated decision systems, likely to protect workers from potential bias or unfair practices. It likely seeks to establish new regulations on the use of AI in hiring and other employment decisions.
